title: Magnificent Manipulation: How the Medici Politicised Public Art
highlights: Renaissance Florence, public spaces served as the physical manifestation of the government’s agenda. Often this took the form of art, as regimes sought to disseminate a set of ideals via public works. This communicative discourse could  ...    the Medici galvanised their grip of Florence, their artistic patronage became a key tool in cultivating public support.
